purpose: Overlay semantic contract classes on top of support_matrix.json status labels. The support taxonomy answers whether an exported symbol has a native/raw-syscall implementation class; this overlay records representative cases where that status is not equivalent to full POSIX/GNU/glibc semantic parity.

semantic_contract_classes.full_semantics: Behavior is intended to match the relevant POSIX, ISO C, GNU, or glibc contract for the supported scope and has fixture/proof/perf evidence elsewhere.
semantic_contract_classes.compat_noop: A symbol intentionally accepts the call and returns success or no observable action for compatibility, but does not implement the full upstream behavior.
semantic_contract_classes.deterministic_fallback: A symbol returns a deterministic fallback/error/fail-stop contract rather than the complete upstream behavior.
semantic_contract_classes.limited_bootstrap: A subsystem path implements a useful bootstrap subset while broader upstream semantics remain deferred.
semantic_contract_classes.unsupported_platform_contract: A historical or non-Linux surface deliberately returns ENOSYS or an equivalent unavailable status.
semantic_contract_classes.proof_gap: The status taxonomy may be correct, but there is insufficient fixture/proof/performance evidence for release-level parity claims.

coverage_policy.why_not_exhaustive: This artifact was created from the current reality-check pass and records high-signal representative drift between taxonomy and semantic completeness. It is intended to become exhaustive in a follow-up gate.
coverage_policy.release_blocker: Do not treat support_matrix.json Stub=0 as proof of full replacement semantics until this overlay is exhaustive and gated.

audited_entries.evidence: RelocationSupport keeps a Stub variant described as documented deterministic behavior even while exported-symbol status counts report Stub=0.
audited_entries.required_followup: Add per-relocation support coverage and gate any README replacement claims on relocation class coverage, not only exported-symbol taxonomy.

audited_entries.evidence: Cancellation register/unregister helpers are documented no-op stubs and __pthread_unwind_next aborts.
audited_entries.required_followup: Split implemented symbol presence from cancellation semantics; add cancellation cleanup/unwind fixtures before any L1 replacement promotion.

audited_entries.evidence: DES encrypt/decrypt helpers are intentionally documented as no-op because DES support is obsolete and unsupported.
audited_entries.required_followup: Keep no-op contract explicit in conformance fixtures and exclude it from full crypt semantic parity counts unless DES behavior is implemented.

audited_entries.surface: NSS files module private plugin entries
audited_entries.evidence: Private NSS plugin entry families return SUCCESS/NOTFOUND/ENOENT style deterministic contracts without opening or parsing the full backing NSS data source.
audited_entries.required_followup: Create an exhaustive NSS private-entry inventory and fixtures that distinguish bootstrap fallthrough behavior from full files/compat/hesiod/systemd backends.

audited_entries.evidence: Code documents direct file parsing and unavailable-style NSS dispatch stubs instead of a complete glibc NSS module system.
audited_entries.required_followup: Gate NSS maturity on nsswitch, module ordering, backend fallthrough, concurrency, and cache-coherence fixtures.

audited_entries.evidence: The variadic obstack helpers set ENOSYS rather than implementing obstack formatting semantics.
audited_entries.required_followup: Add obstack layout and formatting fixtures or reclassify these rows away from full semantic parity.

audited_entries.evidence: Internal stdio vtable helpers are documented deterministic fallback stubs and set ENOSYS.
audited_entries.required_followup: Separate link-compatibility from libio semantic parity and add fixture coverage for reachable _IO helper paths.

audited_entries.surface: removed or unsupported historical syscall wrappers
audited_entries.evidence: Removed or unsupported Linux interfaces deliberately return ENOSYS-style contracts.
audited_entries.required_followup: Keep these rows explicit as unsupported platform contracts and ensure they do not count toward behavior-complete replacement claims.

audited_entries.evidence: Malloc debug hooks are documented as safe no-ops because the membrane supplies allocation safety rather than glibc mcheck/mtrace behavior.
audited_entries.required_followup: Track these as membrane-substituted compatibility contracts with explicit tests and exclude them from glibc debug-hook parity counts.

audited_entries.surface: GNU argp help and reporting helpers
audited_entries.evidence: Argp help/reporting helpers are documented no-op stubs.
audited_entries.required_followup: Add argp parser/help fixtures or classify this family as link-compatible only.

audited_summary.blocked_claim: The current support taxonomy can honestly say there are zero rows with status Stub. It cannot honestly say there are zero no-op, fallback, bootstrap, unsupported, or under-proven semantic surfaces.
